page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM, translating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this x86_64 architecture server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and vector operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance, scoring 82697.57 in stress-ng multi-core tests and achieving top-tier status in Geekbench multi-core (21073.0) and PassMark CPU (83409.49) tests. The hardware excels in cryptographic operations, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 87292.07, and multi-threaded decompression, reaching 525.2 GB/sec in bzip3. Memory bandwidth is also robust, delivering 288.74 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 124.06 GB/sec for large-block writes.

For specialized workloads, the c4-highmem-96 shows high efficiency in static web serving throughput (128.55 GB/sec) and prompt processing for large language models, reaching 297.98 tokens/sec on medium 7B models. However, text generation speeds are more moderate, averaging 4.37 tokens/sec on large 70B models. Given its high resource density and dedicated compute allocation, the instance offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile for enterprise databases, memory-cached applications like Redis, and large-scale software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server configured with 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM, delivering 7.75 GB of memory per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs but supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core CPU performance, strong memory bandwidth, and high throughput for static web serving and database operations. It is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale software compilation, and in-memory databases.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise databases and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 96-vCPU server with 744 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise databases and high-throughput multi-threaded computing.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
Top-tier multi-core performance and high-speed memory bandwidth for uncached operations.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector calculations.
No local storage or bundled GPUs, focusing resources entirely on compute and memory.
Best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and web serving.
